sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 1.81 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.1)

statement.latex: The width\/ of a group$G$is, by definition, the smallest cardinal$m = m(G)$with the property that any subgroup of$G$generated by a finite set$S G$is generated by a subset of$S$of cardinality at most$m$. Does a group of finite width satisfy the minimum condition for subgroups? Does a group with the minimum condition for subgroups have finite width? The same questions under the additional condition ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
